ac_text: Mandatory fields name and primary phone must be provided.
title: Customer profile update succeeds when name and primary phone are provided
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates the single business rule that mandatory fields are accepted in the customer profile update flow.
preconditions: Application is deployed and accessible in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Customer_Service_Agent and has edit permission on Customer Profile Management.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10045 and current values Name: Jane Miller, Primary Phone: +1-555-0147, Email: jane.miller@telecom-example.com.
Customer profile edit form is configured to require Name and Primary Phone before save.
steps: Open Customer ID CUST-10045 in the customer profile edit screen.
Enter Name as Jane Miller.
Enter Primary Phone as +1-555-0147.
Click the Save button.
expected_result: The profile update is saved successfully. The confirmation message is displayed. The customer record persists Name as Jane Miller and Primary Phone as +1-555-0147.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Mandatory fields name and primary phone must be provided.
title: Blank name field shows required-field validation and blocks save
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because this test validates one mandatory-field rule at the user interface layer.
preconditions: Application is deployed and accessible in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Customer_Service_Agent and has edit permission on Customer Profile Management.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10046 and current values Name: Robert Hayes, Primary Phone: +1-555-0188.
Customer profile edit form is configured to require Name and Primary Phone before save.
steps: Open Customer ID CUST-10046 in the customer profile edit screen.
Clear the Name field.
Enter Primary Phone as +1-555-0188.
Click the Save button.
expected_result: The save is blocked. The Name field displays a required-field validation message. No profile changes are persisted for Customer ID CUST-10046.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

API endpoint for customer profile update enforces required fields Name and Primary Phone.
steps: Send an update request for Customer ID CUST-10050 with no Name field.
Send an update request for Customer ID CUST-10050 with no Primary Phone field.

ac_text: Email must be unique across active customers.
title: Update customer email to a unique active email and save successfully
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because the test validates the email uniqueness rule within the customer profile update feature only.
preconditions: Application API is deployed and accessible in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Customer_Service_Agent and permission Customer Profile Edit.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10045, active status, and current email john.smith@telcoexample.com.
No active customer record exists with email maria.garcia@telcoexample.com.
test_data: Active duplicate check scope: active customers only
steps: Submit a customer profile update request for Customer ID CUST-10045 with email maria.garcia@telcoexample.com.
Retrieve Customer ID CUST-10045 after the update request completes.
Query active customer records for email maria.garcia@telcoexample.com.
expected_result: HTTP 200 is returned for the update request. Customer ID CUST-10045 persists with email maria.garcia@telcoexample.com. No validation error is returned. The active customer email search returns exactly one record for Customer ID CUST-10045.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Email must be unique across active customers.
title: Allow customer to keep the same email during self-update without uniqueness error
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because the test verifies the uniqueness rule correctly excludes the same active customer record during update.
preconditions: Application API is deployed and accessible in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Customer_Service_Agent and permission Customer Profile Edit.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10047, active status, and email kevin.lee@telcoexample.com.
No other active customer record exists with email kevin.lee@telcoexample.com.
steps: Submit a customer profile update request for Customer ID CUST-10047 with email kevin.lee@telcoexample.com.
Retrieve Customer ID CUST-10047 after the update request completes.
Query active customer records for email kevin.lee@telcoexample.com.
expected_result: HTTP 200 is returned. Customer ID CUST-10047 remains saved with email kevin.lee@telcoexample.com. No uniqueness validation error is returned. The active customer email search returns exactly one record for Customer ID CUST-10047.

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Email must be unique across active customers.
title: Allow update when the same email exists only on an inactive customer record
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because the test validates the database-level uniqueness scope excludes inactive customer records.
preconditions: Application database is available in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Customer_Service_Agent and permission Customer Profile Edit.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10048, active status, and current email old.customer@telcoexample.com.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10049, inactive status, and email archived.customer@telcoexample.com.
steps: Submit a customer profile update request for Customer ID CUST-10048 with email archived.customer@telcoexample.com.
Query the customer profile row for Customer ID CUST-10048.
Query the customer profile row for Customer ID CUST-10049.
expected_result: The update succeeds and the active customer row for CUST-10048 stores archived.customer@telcoexample.com. The inactive customer row for CUST-10049 remains unchanged. No database uniqueness violation occurs because the duplicate email exists only on an inactive record.

data_integrity.reason: Validates stored state or audit accuracy

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result

ac_text: Email must be unique across active customers.

Retrieve Customer ID CUST-10045 after the update request completes.
Query active customer records for Customer ID CUST-10045.
expected_result: HTTP 400 is returned. Response body contains a field-level validation error for email stating that the email must be unique across active customers. Customer ID CUST-10045 retains its original email value. No duplicate active customer email is persisted.

ac_text: Email must be unique across active customers.
title: Reject activation when an inactive customer email collides with an active customer email
classification_rationale: Functional is appropriate because the test validates the exception path for activating a record that violates the active-customer uniqueness rule.
preconditions: Application API is deployed and accessible in the UAT environment.
User account exists with role Customer_Service_Agent and permission Customer Profile Edit.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10051, inactive status, and email shared.email@telcoexample.com.
Customer record exists with Customer ID CUST-10052, active status, and email shared.email@telcoexample.com.
steps: Submit a customer profile update request for Customer ID CUST-10051 to change status to active.
Retrieve Customer ID CUST-10051 after the update request completes.
Query active customer records for email shared.email@telcoexample.com.
expected_result: HTTP 409 is returned. Response body states that the email already exists for an active customer. Customer ID CUST-10051 remains inactive. The active customer email search returns Customer ID CUST-10052 only.

defect_detection.reason: Designed to catch failures or misbehavior

completeness.reason: Has preconditions, steps, test data, and expected result
